domains.description: Array of domain names to enrich (up to 4 domains). Each domain should be a string without www., @ symbol, or similar.

details.description: Array of person detail objects (up to 10 people). Each object can contain firstName, lastName, name, email, hashedEmail, organizationName, domain, id, or linkedinUrl

items.description: Person identification details used for matching.

domain.description: Domain name of the person's employer (e.g., apollo.io). Do not include www. or @

firstName.description: First name of the person. Typically used with lastName

lastName.description: Last name of the person. Typically used with firstName

name.description: Full name of the person. If provided, firstName and lastName are not needed

organizationName.description: Name of the person's employer (current or previous)

items.title: Person Detail

revealPersonalEmails.description: Set to true to enrich all matched people with personal emails. May consume additional credits. Default: false

revealPhoneNumber.description: Set to true to enrich all matched people with phone numbers. Requires webhookUrl. Default: false

webhookUrl.description: Required if revealPhoneNumber is true. URL where Apollo will send phone number data asynchronously

page.description: The page number of the Apollo data to retrieve. Use with perPage for pagination. Example: 4

perPage.description: The number of search results per page. Limiting results per page improves performance. Use with page parameter to navigate through results. Example: 3

sortByField.description: Sort the opportunities by: `amount` (largest deal values first), `is_closed` (closed deals first), or `is_won` (won deals first)

page.description: Page number of Apollo data to retrieve. Use with perPage for pagination. Example: 4

perPage.description: Number of search results per page. Limiting results per page improves endpoint performance. Use with page. Example: 10

domain.description: The domain of the person's employer (e.g., apollo.io). Do not include www. or @

email.description: The email address of the person

firstName.description: The first name of the person (typically used with lastName)

hashedEmail.description: The hashed email (MD5 or SHA-256 format) for privacy-preserving lookups

id.description: The Apollo ID for the person (from previous searches)

lastName.description: The last name of the person (typically used with firstName)

linkedinUrl.description: The URL of the person's LinkedIn profile

name.description: The full name of the person (first and last name separated by space). Use instead of firstName/lastName

organizationName.description: The name of the person's employer (current or previous)

revealPersonalEmails.description: Set to true to reveal personal email addresses (may consume credits). Defaults to false

revealPhoneNumber.description: Set to true to reveal phone numbers (may consume credits, requires webhookUrl). Defaults to false

webhookUrl.description: Required if revealPhoneNumber is true. URL to receive phone number data asynchronously

accountLabelIds.description: Apollo IDs for labels to include in search results. If multiple labels are provided, accounts connected to any of the labels will be included.

accountStageIds.description: Apollo IDs for account stages to include in search results. If multiple stages are provided, accounts matching any of the stages will be included.

page.description: Page number of Apollo data to retrieve. Use with perPage for pagination.

perPage.description: Number of results per page. Use with page for pagination.

qOrganizationName.description: Keywords to narrow the search of accounts by name. Keywords should directly match at least part of an account's name.

sortAscending.description: Set to true to sort in ascending order. Must be used with sortByField. Defaults to false.

sortByField.description: Sort matching accounts by one of: account_last_activity_date, account_created_at, or account_updated_at.

contactLabelIds.description: Apollo IDs for labels to include in search results. Returns contacts connected to any of the labels.

contactStageIds.description: Apollo IDs for contact stages to include in search results. Returns contacts matching any of the stages along with other parameters.

page.description: Page number of Apollo data to retrieve.

perPage.description: Number of search results per page.

qKeywords.description: Keywords to narrow the search of contacts. Can include combinations of names, job titles, employers (company names), and email addresses.

sortAscending.description: Set to true to sort in ascending order. Must be used with sortByField, otherwise sorting logic is not applied. Defaults to false.

sortByField.description: Field to sort matching contacts by.

contactEmailStatus.description: Email verification statuses to filter by. Options: verified, unverified, likely to engage, unavailable

currentlyNotUsingAnyOfTechnologyUids.description: Exclude companies using these technologies. Replace spaces/periods with underscores. Examples: salesforce, google_analytics, wordpress_org

currentlyUsingAllOfTechnologyUids.description: Technologies that current employer uses (must match ALL). Replace spaces/periods with underscores. Examples: salesforce, google_analytics, wordpress_org

currentlyUsingAnyOfTechnologyUids.description: Technologies that current employer uses (matches ANY). Replace spaces/periods with underscores. Examples: salesforce, google_analytics, wordpress_org

includeSimilarTitles.description: Whether to include people with similar job titles. Set to false for strict matches only. Defaults to true

organizationIds.description: Apollo organization IDs to filter by. Each company has a unique ID

organizationJobLocations.description: Locations of jobs being actively recruited by current employer. Examples: atlanta, japan

organizationJobPostedAtRangeMax.description: Latest date when jobs were posted by current employer (YYYY-MM-DD format). Example: 2025-09-25

organizationJobPostedAtRangeMin.description: Earliest date when jobs were posted by current employer (YYYY-MM-DD format). Example: 2025-07-25

organizationLocations.description: Headquarters locations of current employers (cities, states, countries). Examples: texas, tokyo, spain

organizationNumEmployeesRanges.description: Employee count ranges at current employer. Format: min,max. Examples: 1,10; 250,500; 10000,20000

organizationNumJobsRangeMax.description: Maximum number of active job postings at current employer. Examples: 50, 500

organizationNumJobsRangeMin.description: Minimum number of active job postings at current employer. Examples: 50, 500

page.description: Page number for pagination. Use with perPage to navigate results. Example: 4

perPage.description: Number of results per page. Limits results for better performance. Example: 10

personLocations.description: Locations where people live (cities, states, countries). Examples: california, ireland, chicago

personSeniorities.description: Job seniority levels. Options: owner, founder, c_suite, partner, vp, head, director, manager, senior, entry, intern

personTitles.description: Job titles to search for. Results include similar titles even if not exact matches. Example: marketing manager returns content marketing manager

qOrganizationDomainsList.description: Employer domain names (current or previous). Do not include www., @, or similar. Max 1000 domains per request. Examples: apollo.io, microsoft.com

qOrganizationJobTitles.description: Job titles in active postings at current employer. Examples: sales manager, research analyst

revenueRangeMax.description: Maximum annual revenue of current employer (no currency symbols or commas). Examples: 500000, 1500000

revenueRangeMin.description: Minimum annual revenue of current employer (no currency symbols or commas). Examples: 500000, 1500000

openFactorNames.description: Array of open factor names to filter tasks. Filters tasks by specific criteria or categories.

page.description: Page number of Apollo data to retrieve. Use with `perPage` for pagination. Example: 4.

perPage.description: Number of search results per page. Limiting results per page improves endpoint performance. Use with `page`. Example: 10.

sortByField.description: Sort the tasks by a specific field (e.g., `due_at`, `created_at`, `updated_at`).

accounts.description: Array of account attribute objects. Max 100 items per request.

items.description: Account object fields (all optional per documentation).

merged_crm_ids.description: Additional CRM IDs for deduplication. Items are strings.

owner_id.description: Account owner user ID (BSON::ObjectId). Defaults to current user if not provided.

typed_custom_fields.description: Custom field values as key-value pairs where key is field_id and value is field_value. additionalProperties are strings.
typed_custom_fields.title: Typed Custom Fields

items.title: Account Attributes

append_label_names.description: Array of label names to add to ALL accounts in this request.

run_dedupe.description: Enable aggressive deduplication. Default: false. When true, matches by CRM IDs and domain, organization_id, and name. Existing matches are still returned without modification.

account_attributes.description: Array of account objects with individual updates.

id.description: Required. The account ID to update.

owner_id.description: The Apollo user ID to assign as owner.

typed_custom_fields.description: Custom field values as key-value pairs. Keys are custom field IDs; values are strings.
typed_custom_fields.title: Typed Custom Fields

account_ids.description: Array of account IDs to update with the same values. Limit: < 1000 items.

account_stage_id.description: When using account_ids, applies this stage ID to all accounts in account_ids.

async.description: If true, processes updates asynchronously. Only available when using account_ids; not supported with account_attributes.

name.description: When using account_ids, applies this name to all accounts in account_ids.

owner_id.description: When using account_ids, applies this Apollo user ID as owner to all accounts in account_ids.

account_stage_id.description: Apollo ID of the account stage. Retrieve via List Account Stages. If omitted, Apollo assigns a stage based on your team settings. Example: 6095a710bd01d100a506d4b9.

domain.description: Account domain without www. Example: apollo.io or microsoft.com.

name.description: Human-readable account name. Example: The Irish Copywriters.

owner_id.description: Apollo user ID for the account owner. Retrieve via Get a List of Users. Example: 66302798d03b9601c7934ebf.

phone.description: Primary phone number (any format; Apollo sanitizes). Examples: 555-303-1234, +44 7911 123456.

raw_address.description: Corporate location text; Apollo maps to a predefined location. Examples: Belfield, Dublin 4, Ireland; Dallas, United States.

typed_custom_fields.description: Key/value map of custom field IDs to values. (OpenAPI indicates values as strings via additionalProperties.)
typed_custom_fields.title: Typed Custom Fields

headers.description: Request headers for the API call.
headers.title: Headers

account_label_ids.description: Apollo IDs for labels to include. If multiple are provided, accounts connected to any label are included (in combination with other filters).

account_stage_ids.description: Apollo IDs for account stages to include. If multiple are provided, accounts matching any stage are included (in combination with other filters).

page.description: Page number to retrieve (int32). Use with per_page to navigate results and improve performance.

per_page.description: Results per page (int32). Docs note a limit of 100 records per page.

q_organization_name.description: Keywords to narrow the search of accounts in your team’s Apollo account. Matches account names only (not other fields).

sort_ascending.description: Default: false. Set true to sort ascending. Must be used with sort_by_field or sorting is not applied.

query.description: Optional query parameters used to log the phone call record. Per documentation, these must be sent in the URL query string (not in the body).

account_id.description: Associate the call with an account. Use the “Search for Accounts” endpoint to retrieve account IDs.

contact_id.description: Designate the contact that was called. Use the “Search for Contacts” endpoint to retrieve contact IDs.

duration.description: The duration of the call in seconds (not minutes).

end_time.description: The time when the call ended (ISO 8601). Apollo uses GMT by default; include an offset if needed.

from_number.description: The phone number that dialed you.

logged.description: Set to true if you want to create an individual record for the phone call in Apollo.

note.description: Add a note to the call record.

phone_call_outcome_id.description: Assign a call outcome to the record. Outcomes are workspace-specific; you can find the ID in Apollo UI URLs when filtering by Disposition.

phone_call_purpose_id.description: Assign a call purpose to the record. Purposes are workspace-specific; you can find the ID in Apollo UI URLs when filtering by Purpose.

start_time.description: The time when the call started (ISO 8601). Apollo uses GMT by default; include an offset if needed.

status.description: The status of the phone call.

to_number.description: The phone number that you dialed.

user_id[].description: Designate the caller in your team's Apollo account. Use the “Get a List of Users” endpoint to retrieve user IDs.

headers.description: Request headers for this endpoint. Do not include authentication headers in this specification.
headers.title: Search Phone Calls Headers

query.description: Optional filters and pagination parameters sent in the URL query string.

contact_label_ids[].description: Filter calls that included contacts with specific contact label IDs. Can provide multiple IDs.

date_range[max].description: Upper bound of the date range to search. Use with `date_range[min]`. Must be after `date_range[min]`. Format: `YYYY-MM-DD`. Example: `2025-06-12`.

date_range[min].description: Lower bound of the date range to search. Use with `date_range[max]`. Must be before `date_range[max]`. Format: `YYYY-MM-DD`. Example: `2025-04-01`.

duration[max].description: Upper bound for call duration in seconds. Use with `duration[min]`. Must be larger than `duration[min]`. Example: `180`.

duration[min].description: Lower bound for call duration in seconds. Use with `duration[max]`. Example: `30`.

inbound.description: Filter by call direction: `incoming` (prospect called your team) or `outgoing` (your team called the prospect).

page.description: Page number to retrieve. Use with `per_page`. Example: `4`.

per_page.description: Number of results per page. Use with `page`. Example: `10`.

phone_call_outcome_ids[].description: Filter by call outcome IDs (account-specific). Example: `6095a710bd01d100a506d4c5`.

phone_call_purpose_ids[].description: Filter by call purpose IDs (account-specific). Example: `6095a710bd01d100a506d4cf`.

q_keywords.description: Keywords to narrow search. Example: `marketing conference attendees`.

user_ids[].description: Filter calls that included specific users in your Apollo account. Can provide multiple IDs. Example: `67e33d527de088000daa60c4`.

path.description: Parameters to be substituted into the URL path.

contact_id.description: The Apollo ID for the contact you want to view. Example: `66e34b81740c50074e3d1bd4`. To find IDs, use the “Search for Contacts” endpoint and read the contact `id`.

path.title: Path Parameters

account_id.description: ID of the target account (company) in Apollo. Find via Organization Search (`organization_id`). Example: `5e66b6381e05b4008c8331b8`.

amount.description: Monetary value as a string. Do not include commas or currency symbols; commas cause the amount to be left blank. Example: `55123478`.

closed_date.description: Estimated close date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format. Example: `2025-10-30`.

name.description: Name of the deal (human-readable). Example: `Massive Q3 Deal`.

opportunity_stage_id.description: ID of the deal stage in your Apollo team. Retrieve via List Deal Stages. Example: `6095a710bd01d100a506d4bd`.

owner_id.description: ID of the deal owner in your Apollo team. Retrieve via Get a List of Users. Example: `66302798d03b9601c7934ebf`.

typed_custom_fields.description: Key/value map of custom field IDs to values. Custom fields are unique per team; retrieve IDs/types via Get a List of All Custom Fields. Example: `{"60c39ed82bd02f01154c470a":"2025-08-07"}`.
typed_custom_fields.title: Typed Custom Fields

query.description: Optional pagination and sorting parameters.

page.description: Page number to retrieve. Use with per_page to navigate results and improve performance. Example: 4.

per_page.description: Number of results per page. Limiting results improves performance. Example: 10.

sort_by_field.description: Sort deals by one of the following fields: amount (largest deal values first), is_closed (closed deals first), is_won (won deals first).

query.title: Query Parameters

amount.description: Deal monetary value as a string. Do not include commas or currency symbols (commas are not accepted and may leave amount blank). Currency is derived from Apollo account settings. Example: 55123478.

closed_date.description: Estimated close date formatted as YYYY-MM-DD. Example: 2025-10-30.

name.description: Human-readable deal name. Example: Massive Q3 Deal.

opportunity_stage_id.description: Deal stage ID. Use “List Deal Stages” to find stage id values. Example: 6095a710bd01d100a506d4bd.

owner_id.description: Deal owner’s Apollo user ID. Use “Get a List of Users” to find IDs. Example: 66302798d03b9601c7934ebf.

typed_custom_fields.description: Key/value map for custom fields. Custom field IDs and types are unique to your team. Use “Get a List of All Custom Fields” to find field IDs and valid value types (e.g., picklist entry IDs). Example: { "60c39ed82bd02f01154c470a": "2025-08-07" }.
typed_custom_fields.title: Typed Custom Fields

opportunity_id.description: The ID of the deal (opportunity) to view. Example: `66e09ea8e3cfcf01b2208ec7`. To find IDs, use the “List All Deals” endpoint and use the `id` value of the desired deal.

query.description: Query parameters to send in the URL (not in the body).

domains[].description: The domain of each company to enrich. Required. Do not include www., the @ symbol, or similar. Limit: cannot be more than 10 domains.

hashed_email.description: Hashed email in MD5 or SHA-256 format.

name.description: Full name (typically first + last). Example: tim zheng.

organization_name.description: Employer name (current or previous). Example: apollo.

description: [APOLLO] Enrich a Person (People Enrichment) on apollo

query.description: All inputs for this endpoint are provided as query parameters (not in the request body).

domain.description: Employer domain (current or previous). Do not include www., @, etc. Example: apollo.io.

first_name.description: Person’s first name. Typically used with last_name. Example: tim.

hashed_email.description: MD5 or SHA-256 hash of the person’s email.

last_name.description: Person’s last name. Typically used with first_name. Example: zheng.

name.description: Full name (usually first + last). If used, you do not need first_name/last_name. Example: tim zheng.

organization_name.description: Name of employer (current or previous). Example: apollo.

reveal_personal_emails.description: Set to true to reveal personal emails (may consume credits). Default: false.

reveal_phone_number.description: Set to true to reveal all available phone numbers (may consume credits). Default: false. If true, webhook_url is required.

run_waterfall_email.description: Set to true to enable email waterfall enrichment. Default: false.

run_waterfall_phone.description: Set to true to enable phone waterfall enrichment. Default: false.

webhook_url.description: Required if reveal_phone_number=true; otherwise do not use. HTTPS endpoint where Apollo sends a JSON webhook response with the requested phone number details.

label.description: Name of the custom field you want to create. Example: `Test Name`

max_length.description: Max length for text-based fields (as provided in schema).

modality.description: The modality (object type) the field belongs to. Allowed values: `contact`, `account`, `opportunity`.

type.description: The data type of the custom field. Allowed values: `string`, `textarea`, `number`, `date`, `datetime`, `boolean`.

description: [APOLLO] List All Custom Fields (Deprecated) on apollo

Accept.description: Indicates the client expects a JSON response.

query.description: Optional pagination parameters.

page.description: The page number of results to retrieve. Example: 4.

per_page.description: Number of results per page. Using smaller values can improve performance. Example: 10.

query.title: Query Parameters

name: pica_apollo_get_complete_organization_info
description: [APOLLO] Get Complete Organization Info on apollo

id.description: The Apollo ID for the organization you want to research. Example: `5e66b6381e05b4008c8331b8`.

name: pica_apollo_search_news_articles_for_companies
description: [APOLLO] Search News Articles for Companies on apollo

query.description: Query parameters to be sent in the URL (not in the request body). Bracket notation keys are used as documented.

categories[].description: Filter to specific categories/sub-categories of news. Examples: `hires`, `investment`, `contract`.

organization_ids[].description: The Apollo IDs for the companies you want included in results. Example: `5e66b6381e05b4008c8331b8`. To find IDs, use the Organization Search endpoint and read `organization_id`.

page.description: Page number to retrieve. Use with `per_page`. Example: `4`.

per_page.description: Number of results per page (smaller improves performance). Use with `page`. Example: `10`.

published_at[max].description: Upper bound (inclusive) for published date range. Format: `YYYY-MM-DD`. Use with `published_at[min]`. Must be after `published_at[min]`. Example: `2025-05-15`.

published_at[min].description: Lower bound (inclusive) for published date range. Format: `YYYY-MM-DD`. Use with `published_at[max]`. Must be before `published_at[max]`. Example: `2025-02-15`.

query.title: Query Parameters

name: pica_apollo_list_an_organization_s_job_postings
description: [APOLLO] List an Organization’s Job Postings on apollo

headers.description: HTTP headers to send with the request.
headers.title: Headers

path.description: Parameters that must be embedded in the URL path.

organization_id.description: The organization ID of the company for which you want job postings. Example: `5e66b6381e05b4008c8331b8`.

path.title: Path Parameters

query.description: Optional URL query parameters for pagination.

page.description: Page number to retrieve. Use with `per_page` for navigable results and improved performance. Example: `4`.

per_page.description: Number of results per page (helps performance). Use with `page`. Example: `10`.

query.title: Query Parameters

query.description: All filters for this POST endpoint must be provided as query parameters (not in the body).

currently_using_any_of_technology_uids[].description: Filter by technologies currently used. Apollo supports 1,500+ technologies. Use underscores (`_`) to replace spaces/periods as listed in Apollo’s supported technologies CSV. Examples: `salesforce`, `google_analytics`, `wordpress_org`.

latest_funding_amount_range[max].description: Max amount for the most recent funding round. Use with `latest_funding_amount_range[min]`. No currency symbols/commas/decimals. Examples: `5000000`, `15000000`.

latest_funding_amount_range[min].description: Min amount for the most recent funding round. Use with `latest_funding_amount_range[max]`. No currency symbols/commas/decimals. Examples: `5000000`, `15000000`.

latest_funding_date_range[max].description: Latest date of most recent funding round (YYYY-MM-DD). Use with `latest_funding_date_range[min]`. Example: `2025-09-25`.

latest_funding_date_range[min].description: Earliest date of most recent funding round (YYYY-MM-DD). Use with `latest_funding_date_range[max]`. Example: `2025-07-25`.

organization_ids[].description: Include only these Apollo organization IDs. Example: `5e66b6381e05b4008c8331b8`.

organization_job_locations[].description: Locations of active job postings. Examples: `atlanta`, `japan`.

organization_job_posted_at_range[max].description: Latest job posted date (YYYY-MM-DD). Use with `organization_job_posted_at_range[min]`. Example: `2025-09-25`.

organization_job_posted_at_range[min].description: Earliest job posted date (YYYY-MM-DD). Use with `organization_job_posted_at_range[max]`. Example: `2025-07-25`.

organization_locations[].description: HQ location filter (cities, US states, countries). Results are based on HQ location (not office locations). Examples: `texas`, `tokyo`, `spain`.

organization_not_locations[].description: Exclude organizations by HQ location. Examples: `minnesota`, `ireland`, `seoul`.

organization_num_employees_ranges[].description: Employee count ranges. Each range is "min,max" (comma-separated, no spaces). You can pass multiple ranges. Examples: `1,10`, `250,500`, `10000,20000`.

organization_num_jobs_range[max].description: Maximum number of active job postings. Use with `organization_num_jobs_range[min]`. Examples: `50`, `500`.

organization_num_jobs_range[min].description: Minimum number of active job postings. Use with `organization_num_jobs_range[max]`. Examples: `50`, `500`.

q_organization_domains_list[].description: Domain(s) for the organization. Do not include `www.`, `@`, etc. Accepts up to 1,000 domains per request. Examples: `apollo.io`, `microsoft.com`.

q_organization_job_titles[].description: Job titles found in active job postings at the company. Examples: `sales manager`, `research analyst`.

q_organization_keyword_tags[].description: Filter by keyword tags associated with companies. Examples: `mining`, `sales strategy`, `consulting`.

q_organization_name.description: Filter by organization name (partial matches supported). Example: `apollo` or `mining`.

revenue_range[max].description: Maximum revenue. No currency symbols/commas/decimals. Example: `50000000`.

revenue_range[min].description: Minimum revenue. No currency symbols/commas/decimals. Example: `300000`.

total_funding_range[max].description: Max total funding across all rounds. Use with `total_funding_range[min]`. No currency symbols/commas/decimals. Examples: `50000000`, `350000000`.

total_funding_range[min].description: Min total funding across all rounds. Use with `total_funding_range[max]`. No currency symbols/commas/decimals. Examples: `50000000`, `350000000`.

query.title: Query Parameters

name: pica_apollo_people_api_search_net_new_people_prospecting
description: [APOLLO] People API Search (Net-New People Prospecting) on apollo

query.description: Filters must be sent as query parameters in the URL (not in the body).

currently_not_using_any_of_technology_ui.description: Exclude employers using any specified technologies. Use underscores instead of spaces/periods.

currently_using_all_of_technology_uids[].description: Filter for employers using all specified technologies. Use underscores instead of spaces/periods.

currently_using_any_of_technology_uids[].description: Filter for employers using any specified technologies. Use underscores instead of spaces/periods.

include_similar_titles.description: Whether to include people with titles similar to person_titles[]. Set to false to return only strict matches when using person_titles[].

organization_job_locations[].description: Locations of jobs being actively recruited by the employer.

organization_job_posted_at_range[max].description: Latest date when jobs were posted. Use with organization_job_posted_at_range[min].

organization_job_posted_at_range[min].description: Earliest date when jobs were posted. Use with organization_job_posted_at_range[max].

organization_locations[].description: HQ location of the person’s current employer (cities, states, countries). If a company has multiple offices, filtering is still based on HQ.

organization_num_jobs_range[max].description: Maximum number of active job postings at the employer. Use with organization_num_jobs_range[min].

organization_num_jobs_range[min].description: Minimum number of active job postings at the employer. Use with organization_num_jobs_range[max].

person_locations[].description: Where people live (cities, US states, countries). To filter by employer HQ location instead, use organization_locations[].

person_seniorities[].description: Seniority levels for a person’s current job title. Matches if any one seniority matches.

person_titles[].description: Job titles held by the people you want to find. People only need to match 1 title. Adding more expands results. Also matches similar terms (e.g., marketing manager may match content marketing manager).

q_keywords.description: A string of words over which to filter results.

q_organization_domains_list[].description: Employer domain(s) (current or previous). Do not include www. or @. Up to 1,000 domains per request.

q_organization_job_titles[].description: Job titles listed in active job postings at the person’s current employer.

revenue_range[max].description: Maximum employer revenue. Use with revenue_range[min]. No currency symbols/commas/decimals.

revenue_range[min].description: Minimum employer revenue. Use with revenue_range[max]. No currency symbols/commas/decimals.

query.title: Query parameters

name: pica_apollo_activate_a_sequence_approve_a_sequence
description: [APOLLO] Activate a Sequence (Approve a Sequence) on apollo

sequence_id.description: The Apollo ID for the sequence you want to activate. Example: `66e9e215ece19801b219997f`.

name: pica_apollo_add_contacts_to_a_sequence
description: [APOLLO] Add Contacts to a Sequence on apollo

sequence_id.description: The Apollo ID for the sequence to which you want to add contacts. Example: `66e9e215ece19801b219997f`.

query.description: Query parameters for this action. `emailer_campaign_id` and `send_email_from_email_account_id` are required. Either `contact_ids[]` or `label_names[]` must be provided.

add_if_in_queue.description: Set to `true` to add contacts even if they are currently in the queue for processing. Default: `false`.

auto_unpause_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime when paused contacts should be automatically unpaused. Must be used with `status=paused`.

contact_ids[].description: Apollo IDs for contacts to add. Example item: `66e34b81740c50074e3d1bd4`. Note: Either `contact_ids[]` or `label_names[]` must be provided.

contact_verification_skipped.description: Set to `true` to skip contact verification during the addition process. Default: `false`.

contacts_without_ownership_permission.description: Set to `true` to add contacts even if you don't have ownership permission for them. Default: `false`.

emailer_campaign_id.description: The same ID as the `sequence_id`. Example: `66e9e215ece19801b219997f`.

label_names[].description: Alternative to `contact_ids[]`. Contacts with these label names will be added. Note: Either `contact_ids[]` or `label_names[]` must be provided.

send_email_from_email_account_id.description: Apollo ID for the email account used to send emails to the added contacts. Example: `6633baaece5fbd01c791d7ca`.

send_email_from_email_address.description: Optional specific email address to send from within the email account.

sequence_active_in_other_campaigns.description: Set to `true` to add contacts even if they have been added to other sequences (does not differentiate active vs paused). Default: `false`.

sequence_finished_in_other_campaigns.description: Set to `true` to add contacts if they are marked as `finished` in another sequence. Default: `false`.

sequence_job_change.description: Set to `true` to add contacts even if they recently changed jobs. Default: `false`.

sequence_no_email.description: Set to `true` to add contacts even if they do not have an email address. Default: `false`.

sequence_same_company_in_same_campaign.description: Set to `true` to add contacts even if other contacts from the same company are already in the sequence. Default: `false`.

sequence_unverified_email.description: Set to `true` to add contacts if they have an unverified email address. Default: `false`.

status.description: Initial status for added contacts. Enum: `active`, `paused`. If `paused` with `auto_unpause_at`, enables scheduled addition.

user_id.description: Apollo user ID of the user taking the action (for activity log). Example: `66302798d03b9601c7934ebf`.

query.title: Query Parameters

sequence_id.description: The Apollo ID for the sequence you want to archive. Example: `66e9e215ece19801b219997f`.

name: pica_apollo_check_email_stats_for_an_outreach_email
description: [APOLLO] Check Email Stats for an Outreach Email on apollo

id.description: The ID of the outreach email message you want to view (example: `684b2203a2ce950021cbf730`).

_pica_metadata.action_title: Check Email Stats for an Outreach Email

name: pica_apollo_deactivate_a_sequence_abort_an_emailer_campaign
description: [APOLLO] Deactivate a Sequence (Abort an Emailer Campaign) on apollo

sequence_id.description: The Apollo ID for the sequence you want to deactivate. Example: `66e9e215ece19801b219997f`. To find IDs, use the “Search for Sequences” endpoint and read the `id` field.

_pica_metadata.action_title: Deactivate a Sequence (Abort an Emailer Campaign)

query.description: Optional filters and pagination controls. Query parameters must be sent in the URL (not in the body).

email_account_id_and_aliases.description: Filter by email account id and aliases.

emailer_campaign_ids[].description: Include only emails in specific sequences (campaigns). You can pass multiple IDs.

emailer_message_date_range[max].description: Upper bound date (format YYYY-MM-DD). Must be after min. Use with [min] and emailer_message_date_range_mode.

emailer_message_date_range[min].description: Lower bound date (format YYYY-MM-DD). Must be before max. Use with [max] and emailer_message_date_range_mode.

emailer_message_date_range_mode.description: Used with emailer_message_date_range[max] and [min]. Choose what the range applies to.

emailer_message_reply_classes[].description: Filter by reply sentiment/classification. You can pass multiple values.

emailer_message_stats[].description: Filter by current email status/engagement. You can pass multiple values.

not_emailer_campaign_ids[].description: Exclude emails from specific sequences (campaigns). You can pass multiple IDs.

not_sent_reason_cds[].description: Filter by reason message was not sent. You can pass multiple values.

page.description: Page number to retrieve. Use with per_page.

per_page.description: Results per page. Endpoint limit is 100 per page.

q_keywords.description: Keyword search across email content (partial match).

user_ids[].description: Filter emails sent by specific Apollo users (IDs in your team account).

query.title: Search Outreach Emails Query Parameters

body.description: This endpoint’s documentation does not specify any request body fields. Send an empty JSON object {} if your HTTP client requires a body for POST.
body.title: Request Body

query.description: Optional query parameters for searching and pagination. Must be sent in the URL query string, not in the request body.

page.description: Page number of results to retrieve. Use with per_page for pagination/performance. Example: "4".

per_page.description: Number of results per page (limiting improves performance). Use with page. Example: "10".

q_name.description: Keywords to narrow the search by sequence name only. Keywords should match at least part of a sequence’s name. Example: "marketing conference attendees".

query.title: Query Parameters

name: pica_apollo_update_contact_status_in_a_sequence
description: [APOLLO] Update Contact Status in a Sequence on apollo

query.description: Although the method is POST, inputs are provided as query parameters (not a JSON request body).

contact_ids[].description: The Apollo IDs for the contacts whose sequence status you want to update.

emailer_campaign_ids[].description: The Apollo IDs for the sequences to update. If multiple are provided, the contacts’ statuses are updated across all specified sequences.

query.title: Query Parameters

_pica_metadata.action_title: Update Contact Status in a Sequence

contact_ids.description: Apollo contact IDs to receive the action. A separate task is created for each contact ID.

note.description: Human-readable description providing context for the task owner.

status.description: Status of the task being created. Examples/notes: use scheduled for future tasks; for already completed tasks use completed or skipped.

user_id.description: ID of the task owner within your Apollo team account (the user who will take action). Example: 66302798d03b9601c7934ebf.

contact_id.description: The Apollo ID for the contact the task is associated with. Example: `66e34b81740c50074e3d1bd4`.

due_at.description: Due date/time in ISO 8601 / RFC3339 format.

note.description: Human-readable description/context for the task. Recommended but not required.

priority.description: Priority of the task. Allowed values: `high`, `medium`, `low`. Default: `medium`.

status.description: Status of the task. Guidance: use `scheduled` for future-facing tasks; for already completed tasks use `completed` or `skipped`.

title.description: Title for the task. If omitted, Apollo auto-generates a title based on task type and contact name.

user_id.description: The ID for the task owner within your Apollo account (the user who will take action). Example: `66302798d03b9601c7934ebf`.

query.description: Optional query string parameters. Per spec, query parameters must be sent in the URL and not in the body.

open_factor_names[].description: Use `task_types` to return a count of tasks by task type. When used, the response includes a `faceting.task_types` array with `count` per task type.

page.description: Page number to retrieve. Use with `per_page` for navigation/performance. Example: `4`.

per_page.description: Number of results per page. Example: `10`. (Docs note a hard limit of 100/page; requesting too many may trigger a 422 error like `Per page not supported`.)

sort_by_field.description: Sort the tasks by one of: `task_due_at` (most future-dated first), `task_priority` (highest priority first).

query.title: Query Parameters

description: [APOLLO] List Fields in an Apollo Account on apollo

Accept.description: Media type accepted for the response.

query.description: Optional query parameters for filtering results.
query.title: Query Parameters
